data.title: Macy's in charts: Bloomingdale comp sales growth of 3.2%, Bluemercury comp sales up 3.3%
C3.ai, PagerDuty in focus as JP Morgan changes ratings on enterprise software stocks
data.description:
C3.ai (NYSE:AI) and PagerDuty (NYSE:PD) were in focus on Wednesday as J.P. Morgan changed its ratings on several enterprise software stocks going into 2025.

C3.ai and PagerDuty were cut to Underweight from Neutral, as was Sprinklr (NYSE:CXM). Conversely, the research firm kept its Overweight rating on Confluent (NASDAQ:CFLT) and upped its price target to $38 from $35.

C3.ai fell 5.5% in premarket trading, while PagerDuty and Sprinklr dropped 3.2% and 2.5%, respectively. Confluent was up fractionally.

C3.ai

C3.ai was downgraded on the basis that is going through "uneven and subpar growth-plus-margin performance," despite being a part of the artificial intelligence craze, J.P. Morgan analysts said.

"While revenue growth has improved lately, the incremental costs to drive that growth appear high, with incremental margins being essentially flat in the [last 12 months] (+2%) and the current guide for this FY implying an incremental margin of -30%," the analysts wrote.

And while the company has forged a new deal with Microsoft (MSFT), its existing deal Baker Hughes could expire in April 2025 and be an offset.

"While the Microsoft relationship likely dramatically expands its [go-to-market] reach and could be beneficial to growth, we await to see proof points before underwriting its success," the analysts added.

PagerDuty

PagerDuty was cut as J.P. Morgan's analysts said they see "several crosscurrents at play, which makes it difficult to underwrite the long-term success of the company’s multi-product platform play at this point."

"Most importantly, we think we are observing a commoditization of the core product driven by a rapidly evolving competitive landscape, in addition to a lot of large established vendors upstream, such as Observability players and [IT service management] vendors, with strong C-suite relationships developing capabilities such as AIOps and Automations, potentially shifting the power dynamics in the space," the analysts added.

"We think these rapidly evolving fundamental market dynamics could introduce unfavorable contract negotiations with respect to term and pricing, especially around expansion of large deals for PagerDuty, and could likely pressure new customer additions."

Sprinklr

Sprinklr was downgraded as J.P. Morgan's analysts said they see a "balanced risk/reward at the current price levels."

"While we view the changes being introduced by the new CEO as a step in the right direction, especially with respect to the focus on attaining a Rule of 40%, we think these changes will take time to meaningfully reinvigorate growth, and could potentially introduce some near-term execution risk," they wrote.

Confluent

The firm raised its price target on Confluent to $38 from $35, noting that the new 9x EV/CY26E revenue multiple is "in line" with other companies with similar revenue and margin profiles, such as Datadog (DDOG), Zscaler (ZS), MongoDB (MDB), GitLab (GTLB), CyberArk (CYBR), Elastic (ESTC) and JFrog (FROG).

"Confluent’s CY26E revenue growth is slightly higher than peers (22% vs. 21% median for comps), although this is offset by a lower CY26E uFCF margin profile (11% vs. 24% median for comps)," analysts at the firm wrote. "Additionally, we see MDB as a very close comp to CFLT given their open source heritage, proximity to developers, and similar growth and profitability levels."

data.title: Bread Financial card delinquency rate dips in November
data.description:
Bread Financial Holdings' (NYSE:BFH) credit card delinquency rate declined in November, while the percentage of loans that it charged off edged up in November, the company said on Wednesday.

The November delinquency rate of 6.2% fell from 6.4% in October and from 6.5% in the year-ago month. Before the pandemic, in November 2019, the delinquency rate was 5.9%.

The net charge-off rate of 8.0% rose from 7.9% in both October and November 2023, and is well above the November 2019 level of 6.3%.

Credit card and other loans rose 1.1% M/M, to $18.1B from $17.9B at Oct. 31, 2024, and declined 3.7% Y/Y.

data.title: XXIX Metal to change ticker to XXIX
data.description:
• XXIX Metal (OTCQB:QCCUF) said its shares will begin trading on the TSX Venture Exchange as XXIX Metal Corp, and its ticker symbol will change to XXIX.
  • The change in the ticker symbol is due to the firm's rebranding as XXIX Metal, reflecting its recent acquisition of Cuprum and its Thierry copper project, located in Pickle Lake, Ontario.

data.title: Krispy Kreme warns of a material near-term impact from IT hacking incident
data.description:
Krispy Kreme (NASDAQ:DNUT) disclosed in an SEC filing that it was notified on November 29 about unauthorized activity on a portion of its information technology systems. The Charlotte-based company said it immediately began taking steps to investigate, contain, and remediate the incident with the assistance of leading cybersecurity experts.

As of December 11, Krispy Kreme (DNUT) shops globally are open, and consumers can place orders in person, but the chain is experiencing certain operational disruptions, including with online ordering in parts of the United States. Daily fresh deliveries to retail and restaurant partners are uninterrupted.

Krispy Kreme (DNUT) said it working diligently with external cybersecurity experts to respond to and mitigate the impact of the incident, including the restoration of online ordering, and has notified federal law enforcement. As the investigation of the incident is ongoing, the full scope, nature, and impact of the incident are not yet known.

In terms of impact, Krispy Kreme (DNUT) said the incident has had and is reasonably likely to have a material impact on the company’s business operations until recovery efforts are completed. The expected costs related to the incident, including the loss of revenues from digital sales during the recovery period, fees for our cybersecurity experts and other advisors, and costs to restore any impacted systems, are reasonably likely to have a material impact on the company’s results of operations and financial condition.

Krispy Kreme (DNUT) holds cybersecurity insurance that is expected to offset a portion of the costs of the incident. The company does not expect this will have a long-term material impact on its results of operations and financial condition.

Shares of Krispy Kreme (DNUT) were down 1.78% in premarket trading on Wednesday.

data.title: S&P upgrades Quad/Graphics on active debt repayment, improved leverage
data.description:
• S&P Global Ratings upgraded its issuer credit rating for Quad/Graphics (QUAD) to BB- from B+, with a stable outlook, on active debt repayment and improved leverage.
  • The rating agency also raised its issue-level rating on the company's senior secured debt to BB-.
  • The upgrade is driven by Quad's active debt repayment and improved leverage, and strong cost management practices amid ongoing revenue pressures.
  • Considering Quad's rapid debt repayment, S&P now expects the marketing experience company to reduce its reported net leverage to 1.5x (approximately 2.3x on an S&P Global Ratings-adjusted basis) by 2024-end.
  • The stable outlook reflects S&P's expectation that the company's S&P Global Ratings-adjusted leverage will remain below 3x and its free operating cash flow to debt will remain above 15% over the next 12 months.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Patterson jumps 34% on $4.1B Patient Square's take private deal
data.description:
• Patterson Companies (NASDAQ:PDCO) stock jumped 34% in premarket trade, after it said it will be acquired by Patient Square Capital in a $4.1B take private deal.
  • Under the deal, the firm will get $31.35 in cash per share, which is a 35.7% premium to the company's last close on Tuesday.
  • The deal will be funded through a combination of committed equity financing provided by Patient Square Equity Partners, as well as committed debt financing to be led by Citi, UBS Investment Bank, and Wells Fargo Bank N.A.
  • The transaction is expected to close in the fourth quarter of PDCO's fiscal 2025, and after completion it will become a privately held company.
  • Patterson will maintain its headquarters in St. Paul, Minnesota, the company said.

data.description:

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Macy's tops Q3 estimates; lowers FY earnings forecast on accounting error
Nvidia, AMD, Intel invest in Ayar Labs, valuing startup at $1B: report
data.description:
• Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA), AMD (NASDAQ:AMD) and Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) have invested in semiconductor startup Ayar Labs, valuing the company at $1B, Bloomberg reported.
  • Ayar Labs, which is based in San Jose, uses light that allows for data to be transferred between processors faster and could boost the trio's artificial intelligence accelerators by making them more efficient.
  • “The AI workload is really breaking the back of the existing hardware infrastructure, especially in interconnects,” Ayar Labs Chief Executive Mark Wade told the news outlet. “We’ve come up with a way to replace those electrical interconnects.”
  • Altogether, Ayar Labs raised $155M in new funding, with Nvidia, AMD Ventures and Intel Capital joining the round. Leading the round were Light Street Capital and Advent Global Opportunities, the news outlet reported.
  • Valued at more than $3T, Nvidia has upped the use of its balance sheet to invest in companies in recent memory. Earlier this month, Nvidia was among a group of investors that invested in the $700M private placement financing round by Nebius (NBIS). The Jensen Huang-led company also invested in Applied Digital (APLD) in September.
